hero.headline:
Africa doesn't need
to catch up.
It needs to build
differently.
hero.sub: Ideas, systems, and builder profiles for Africa's entrepreneurs and technologists. One rigorous idea, every week. Powered by the belief that 1.4 billion people is the world's greatest untapped asset.

ideas.label: Ideas
ideas.headline: Rigorous thinking for Africa's builders
ideas.body: We take the most important questions about Africa's technological and entrepreneurial future and answer them with depth, not opinion. Every article maps a gap into a structural solution. Every piece of content hands something usable to a builder.

builders.label: Builders
builders.headline: The people constructing Africa's next chapter
builders.body: Not inspiration stories. Decision case studies. We profile the entrepreneurs and technologists building Africa's digital economy — asking not 'how did they succeed?' but 'what specific decision changed everything, and why did it work?'

audio.label: Audio
audio.headline: Every idea, also a conversation
audio.body: Every article on Afrinia is also a focused 6–8 minute audio episode. The Afrinia Brief — distributed on Spotify and Apple Podcasts. Read it at your desk. Listen to it on your commute. The idea travels with you.

belief.quote: "We don't mourn Africa's gaps — we map them into structural solutions. We don't react to headlines — we extract the opportunity inside and hand it to builders. Africa doesn't need to catch up. It needs to build differently — with AI, collective intelligence, and 1.4 billion people as its greatest asset."

builder.title:
The people
doing the work
builder.quote: "I stopped asking what was wrong with Africa and started asking what could be built. That single shift changed everything about how I work."
builder.name: Amara Diallo
builder.bio: In 2021, Amara walked away from a stable consulting career to build a no-code automation platform for West African SMBs. Three years later, it serves 400+ businesses across 11 countries. This is not an inspiration story. This is a decision map.

audio.title:
Every idea.
Also a conversation.
audio.body: Every article on Afrinia is also a focused 6–8 minute audio episode. Read it at your desk. Listen on your commute. The idea travels with you. Available on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

newsletter.headline: One idea.
newsletter.desc: One deeply researched idea for African builders — delivered to your inbox before the week ends. Not a news digest. Not a trend report. One complete thought you can act on. Read by entrepreneurs, technologists, and thinkers across 30+ African countries.
newsletter.promise: No noise. No spam. Unsubscribe any time.
newsletter.successMsg: You're in. Expect the first brief this Thursday.
newsletter.alreadyMsg: You're already subscribed. Check your inbox for the latest brief.
newsletter.errorMsg: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.title: Founder, Afrinia · Builder of Intelligence Systems
hero.lead: "I believe Africa's story is still being written — and that those who build the tools to tell it, shape it."
hero.body: I am a Congolese-born founder, living at the intersection of vision and execution. My journey has moved through continents, industries, and ideas — but one conviction has remained constant: that the African continent is not waiting to be discovered. It is waiting for the right infrastructure to make its intelligence visible to the world.

b1.headline: Intelligence belongs to everyone
b1.body: For too long, the signal that shapes decisions — business intelligence, market data, strategic insight — has been concentrated in systems built for a world that excludes Africa by default. I believe access to real intelligence is not a privilege. It is a foundation.

b2.headline: Language is not a barrier — it is architecture
b2.body: Africa speaks in many tongues. French and English are not just communication choices — they are two distinct ecosystems of trust, of relationship, of commerce. Building bilingually is not a feature. It is a statement of respect.

b3.headline: Africa is not a problem to solve
b3.body: I am not here to fix Africa. I am here to build for it — with the same rigor, elegance, and ambition that any world-class market deserves. The continent's entrepreneurs are not underdogs. They are the protagonists of the next chapter of global business.

b4.headline: Technology must earn trust before it demands attention
b4.body: I have learned — through building, failing, and rebuilding — that no platform is worth launching before it is worth trusting. Every product I ship is a promise. And I do not make promises I cannot keep.

mission.quote: "Afrinia exists because Africa's entrepreneurs deserve to act on intelligence — not operate in its absence."

aspirations.label: Where I am going
aspirations.heading: Building the intelligence layer Africa has always deserved.
aspirations.body: The vision is not modest. And it should not be. What I am building with Afrinia is not a website, not a blog, not a news aggregator. It is the beginning of an intelligence operating system — one that transforms raw signal into strategic clarity, in both languages, for every ambitious mind on this continent.

a1.title: To become Africa's first bilingual business intelligence authority
a1.desc: Afrinia will be the place where Francophone and Anglophone entrepreneurs come not for news, but for the intelligence that turns news into action — curated, contextualised, and built from African sources first.

a2.title: To build a community where the continent thinks together
a2.desc: Beyond content, I want Afrinia to become the gathering place — a bilingual space where the continent's sharpest minds find each other, challenge each other, and build together.

a3.title: To prove that AI can serve Africa on Africa's terms
a3.desc: The intelligence engine I am building will be trained on African realities, not imported assumptions. When it answers, it will answer from within the continent — not from the outside looking in.

coda.text: "I am a husband, a father, and a builder. Everything I create is a reflection of what I believe — that technology, when built with discipline and love, can carry dignity forward."

hero.title: What We Install
hero.subtitle: Three focused service lines — all Google Workspace-native

outreachOS.tagline: Done-with-you or self-install · Runs in your Google account
outreachOS.for: Solo consultants, fractional executives, and small agencies doing B2B sales
outreachOS.outcome: 450+ personalised email touches per month + LinkedIn DM queue — ~10 minutes of daily effort

quickWin.title: QuickWin Automation Install
quickWin.for: Small businesses drowning in repetitive admin

operationsControlRoom.title: Operations Control Room
operationsControlRoom.for: Teams that need structure without Jira/Asana

includes.roleTasks: Role-based task assignment (Sheet UI + triggers + notifications)

configurator.title: Configurator & PDF Engine
configurator.for: Modular products / pricing / BOM / quotes / proposals
configurator.outcome: Configure → price → generate branded PDF in minutes

timeAudit.title: Time Audit (15 min)
timeAudit.description: We identify one repetitive pain point.

install.title: Install
install.description: We build it inside your Google Workspace.

prove.title: Prove
prove.description: You see it run. You own it completely.

software.question: Do we need new software?
software.answer: No. Everything runs inside your existing Google Workspace — Sheets, Drive, Gmail. No new logins, no new subscriptions.

data.question: Do you store our data?
data.answer: No. All data lives in your Google account. We build inside your Workspace; we don't host or store your data.

ai.question: Is AI required?
ai.answer: No. Our systems are logic-first. AI is optional where it adds value (e.g. drafting or summarising); nothing depends on it.

hero.title: Example Systems We've Installed
hero.subtitle: Real workflows we've automated for clients. Each installation is adapted to the specific business.

taskAssignment.title: Role-Based Task Assignment System
taskAssignment.problem: Tasks were tracked informally, ownership was unclear, and follow-ups were missed.
taskAssignment.system: A role-based task assignment system built in Google Sheets with automated reminders, status tracking, and weekly reporting.
taskAssignment.outcome: The team gained clarity without introducing Jira or Asana. Everything runs inside their existing Google Workspace.

configurator.title: Modular Stack Configurator
configurator.problem: Manual configuration led to pricing errors and repeated document creation.
configurator.system: A Google Sheets-based configurator that validates inputs, applies business constraints, and generates branded PDFs automatically.
configurator.outcome: Sales cycles shortened, errors dropped, and outputs became consistent. The system is re-runnable and fully owned by the client.

emailLogger.title: Email → Sheet Auto-Logger + Follow-Up System
emailLogger.problem: Support emails were manually logged into spreadsheets, and follow-ups were often forgotten.
emailLogger.system: Incoming emails are automatically parsed, key fields extracted, and appended to a Google Sheet. Follow-up reminders are sent at +3, +7, and +14 days if no reply is received.
emailLogger.outcome: The team eliminated copy-paste work and never missed a follow-up again.

note.line1: Each installation is adapted to the client's workflow.
note.line2: Logic and structure vary by business.
note.line3: These are examples of patterns we implement — not ready-made products.

hero.title: OutreachOS
hero.subtitle: Automated B2B outreach that runs inside your Google account. One payment. No subscriptions.

problem.title: Most outreach fails for one of two reasons.

tooManual.title: Too manual
tooManual.body: You spend hours researching prospects, writing individual emails, tracking who you contacted. It's a second job. And it still doesn't scale.

tooRobotic.title: Too robotic
tooRobotic.body: Blast campaigns get ignored or flagged as spam. Prospects can tell it's a template. Your reply rate collapses. Your domain reputation suffers.

problem.solution: OutreachOS solves both. Volume AND personalisation. Automatically.

howItWorks.title: Six things OutreachOS does every day.
howItWorks.subtitle: All inside your Google account. Nothing leaves your Drive.

1.title: Finds Prospects
1.desc: Runs targeted searches by job title, industry, and location — automatically cycling through your ideal customer profile combinations.

2.title: Enriches Each Lead
2.desc: Captures name, company, role, and LinkedIn URL for every prospect found — no manual research required.

3.title: Writes Personalised Emails
3.desc: Uses AI to write a unique email for each prospect. Not a template with a name swapped in — a genuinely tailored message.

4.title: Sends via Gmail or Zoho Mail
4.desc: Emails go out from your own dedicated business address — via Gmail or Zoho Mail. Your domain, your reputation, fully under your control.

5.title: Drafts LinkedIn DMs
5.desc: Generates a matching LinkedIn message for each prospect — ready for you to review and send in a 10-minute daily queue.

6.title: Logs Everything
6.desc: Every prospect, email, status, and reply date tracked automatically in your Google Sheet control room.

numbers.title: What OutreachOS delivers in 30 days.
numbers.disclaimer: Based on standard ICP configuration with 15 prospects per daily batch.

items.1: You sell B2B services worth £3,000 or more per deal
items.2: You're a solo consultant, fractional executive, or run a 1–5 person agency
items.3: You use Gmail and Google Workspace already
items.4: You want consistent outreach without hiring a VA or SDR
items.5: You're tired of paying £300+/month for tools that still need a team to run
items.6: You want to own your system — not rent it

notFor.2: You need to send 1,000+ emails per day
notFor.3: You want a fully managed service (see NodeMatics Done-For-You)

items.3: We configure your ICP live with you
